summary: I have been living in the United States for 25 years since I migrated here on October 4th, 1992 from Fiji. My uncle sponsored me and my family to come here, and the immigration process took about twelve years. The United States has given me better job opportunities and a chance to provide a good life for my children, which we couldn't have had in Fiji.

Fiji is where I was born and raised before migrating to the United States. It is a multicultural and overpopulated country with Fijians, Indians, and Japanese living together. Life there was very hard due to poverty, expensive schooling, and scarce job opportunities. My family struggled a lot, and I remember times when we had no money even for food. Fiji is a country of native Fijian people, and many Indians like my family ended up there because of the indenture system.

My roots are from India, and I am proud to be Indian. In 1879, Indian people were shipped from India to Fiji to work on cotton plantations, which is how my ancestors ended up there. I always want my daughter to stay connected to our Indian culture and religion and to be proud of being Indian. India is the origin of my family’s history before they migrated to Fiji.

The Indian indenture system brought my ancestors from India to Fiji in January 1879 to work on cotton plantations. This system caused generations of Indian people to settle in Fiji, shaping the multicultural society there. It explains how my family ended up in Fiji, and it is a significant part of our history and identity.

Australia is one of the countries that many people from Fiji dream of migrating to for better opportunities. Like the United States and New Zealand, it represents hope for a better life away from the poverty and overpopulation of Fiji. Many families seek visas or sponsorships to move to places like Australia to improve their living conditions.

New Zealand is another destination that people from Fiji consider when looking to migrate for better opportunities. Along with the United States and Australia, it offers a chance to escape the hardships of life in Fiji. Many families, including mine, look for any way to get sponsored or obtain visas to move to New Zealand for a better future.
